response.justification: The candidate is a capable full-stack and backend engineer with a genuine software craftsmanship ethos, but critically fails the minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ experience — the resume shows only ~6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, with the primary skill set built around TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. The cloud infrastructure depth required for a cloud compute platform lead is similarly absent; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz reflects product-team infrastructure tooling, not ownership of a cloud compute platform. Without these two core pillars, the role is not a fit regardless of the candidate's other qualities.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship and testing culture: TDD advocate, created open-source D testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and ran multi-module testing/CI workshops
Demonstrated exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O, high-performance caching with DLang)
Long track record of autonomous ownership and initiative: founding GISCollective on Kubernetes, setting up DevOps infrastructure at Altom, independently shipping an Apple TV app
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project), falling dramatically short of the 8-year minimum requirement that is central to this role
No cloud compute platform architecture experience; AWS CDK at Optilyz is product-team infrastructure-as-code, not designing or owning a cloud compute platform
No evidence of technical leadership within an infrastructure or cloud engineering team; mentoring activities are community/workshop-oriented rather than engineering-org leadership
